title: Verify bead state isolation and merge safety
description: Run the full test suite and verify all code-level acceptance criteria from spec-d1a1398c. Specifically check: uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q passes; grep -n '_du_conflicted_files' src/agent_takt/gitutils.py returns no results; grep -n 'untrack bead state' src/agent_takt/gitutils.py returns no results; tests in test_merge_safety.py pass; the commit_all pathspec exclusion test passes; tests in test_gitutils_worktree_exclude.py pass.
acceptance_criteria: uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q passes with no failures
No chore commit logic or 'untrack bead state' string in gitutils.py
commit_all pathspec exclusion unit test in test_scheduler_beads.py passes

handoff_summary.completed: Ran targeted tests (test_gitutils_worktree_exclude.py, test_merge_safety.py, test_scheduler_beads.py). Confirmed both grep checks pass (_du_conflicted_files absent, no 'untrack bead state' string in gitutils.py). Fixed test_preflight_merge_uses_same_merge_driver_wrapper by adding missing 'feature' direction argument to the mock assertion — this test was written before B-6e740d75 added the direction parameter to _merge_with_bead_state_fallback.
handoff_summary.remaining: Three test failures require production code fixes in src/agent_takt/gitutils.py._resolve_bead_state_conflicts: (1) Bug A — line 186 uses '--theirs' for direction='main' but should use '--ours'; both merge paths want the current branch's bead state and 'ours' is always correct. Affects test_final_merge_keeps_main_branch_bead_state_when_both_branches_track_it and test_existing_worktree_is_retrofitted_before_merge. (2) Bug B — DU-status bead conflicts (file deleted on feature, modified on main) crash with 'does not have our version' on git checkout --ours. Need git rm for DU-status files. Affects test_du_conflict_in_bead_state_resolves_via_rm.
handoff_summary.risks: Bug A means every final takt merge (feature→main) picks the feature branch's bead state instead of main's, silently corrupting the canonical bead record on main. Bug B causes GitError on a merge path that was previously protected by _du_conflicted_files (now removed by B-6e740d75) — any real merge with DU bead conflicts will crash.
handoff_summary.next_action: Developer corrective bead must fix src/agent_takt/gitutils.py._resolve_bead_state_conflicts: (1) change line 186 from '--theirs' for direction='main' to '--ours' always; (2) detect DU-status files via git status --porcelain and resolve them with git rm before attempting git checkout --ours on remaining UU files.
handoff_summary.block_reason: Two production code bugs in _resolve_bead_state_conflicts cause 3 test failures. Bug A: checkout_side='--theirs' for direction='main' picks feature-branch bead state instead of main's — should always be '--ours'. Bug B: DU-status bead conflicts (git rm --cached on feature, modified on main) crash with 'does not have our version' — must use git rm for DU files. Both bugs are in src/agent_takt/gitutils.py lines 178-196.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only test_merge_safety.py was modified; production code untouched.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Always use '--ours' in _resolve_bead_state_conflicts regardless of direction, since both merge_branch (on main) and merge_main_into_branch (on feature) want to preserve the current branch's bead state — 'ours' always refers to the current branch.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: 101 of 105 targeted tests pass. 4 pass completely: WriteWorktreeExcludeTests (3), EnsureWorktreeExcludeIntegrationTests (2), WorktreeBeadLeakRegressionTests (2 of 3), MergeMainIntoBranchSaveRestoreTests (5), EndToEndMergeSafetyCycleTests pass. 3 failures all trace to the same _resolve_bead_state_conflicts function. test_scheduler_beads.py pathspec-exclusion test passes.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: DU-conflict handling (Bug B) was explicitly called out in spec known-limitations as a potential issue but was not implemented. The spec's 'workaround' (DU files are rare) is insufficient since test_du_conflict_in_bead_state_resolves_via_rm is an explicit acceptance criterion that must pass.

conflict_risks: None — only test_merge_safety.py was modified; production code untouched.

execution_history.summary: One test synchronization defect fixed (missing direction arg in mock assertion). Two production code bugs in _resolve_bead_state_conflicts cause 3 remaining test failures: wrong checkout side for direction=main, and missing DU-conflict handling via git rm.
Created corrective bead B-997b348e-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B-997b348e-corrective completed

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

**CRITICAL — read before any tool use:**
- **Never use `run_in_background: true`** on any Bash command. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is captured before the structured verdict is emitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- **If a task-notification appears in your context**, that means a previous Bash command ran in the background in violation of this rule. Do not treat the notification as a sign that work is done. Re-run the test command synchronously (without `run_in_background`) and emit the JSON verdict block as your final response.
- **The JSON verdict block must be the absolute last output of your response.** Never end with prose, a status summary, or a notification acknowledgement. The orchestrator parses your final message as JSON and will fail if it is not valid JSON.

---

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Target individual test files or modules. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide. Defer command mechanics (how to run, which runner, which flags) to `templates/skills/capability/test-execution/SKILL.md`.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always run targeted, scoped commands. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. (See CRITICAL section above.)
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing or running any tests, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable testing knowledge** — patterns, pitfalls, or edge cases that would benefit future testers of this codebase.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.

**FINAL REMINDER: The JSON verdict block must be the last thing you output. Do not add any text, prose, or acknowledgement after it.**
